methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $56M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Formic is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Saman Farid and Misa Ilkhechi, having raised $56M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a broader robotics and manufacturing sector universe comprising one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity. However, same-stage and adjacent-stage peer filtering within this cohort yielded insufficient datapoints for reliable median-based valuation.

The Stage-Progression method applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement against the $56M total raised baseline, implying a valuation of $112M. This approach captures the observed step-function appreciation between funding rounds in the sector. Peer-median triangulation was deemed inapplicable due to the narrow peer set failing to meet the three-datapoint threshold when filtered by stage and valuation tier, rendering broad-sector medians unrepresentative of Formic's specific profile.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $95M to $129M, with a median implied value of $112M. Key uncertainties include the limited comparable set available at Formic's specific stage and the reliance on a single methodological input. Any valuation exercise here should be treated as provisional pending either acquisition of additional comparable data or disclosure of Formic's current revenue, margins, or other operating metrics that would support alternative approaches.
